stem: The region bounded by the curve \( y = [[m]]x \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \) is revolved about the \( y \)-axis. Using cylindrical shells, find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: A shell at radius \( x \) has height \( [[m]]x \), so the volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} 2\pi x\,([[m]]x)\,dx = \dfrac{2\pi[[m]]}{3}\left([[b]]^{3}-[[a]]^{3}\right) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the disk method \( \pi\int (\,[[m]]x)^{2}dx \) instead of shells.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This omits the shell radius \( x \), integrating \( 2\pi\,([[m]]x) \).
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( 2\pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region bounded by the curve \( x = [[m]]y \), the \( y \)-axis, and the horizontal lines \( y = [[a]] \) and \( y = [[b]] \) is revolved about the \( x \)-axis. Using cylindrical shells, find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Because the axis of revolution is horizontal, each shell is stacked along \( y \) with radius \( y \) and height \( [[m]]y \), so the volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} 2\pi y\,([[m]]y)\,dy = \dfrac{2\pi[[m]]}{3}\left([[b]]^{3}-[[a]]^{3}\right) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the disk method instead of shells.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This omits the shell radius \( y \), integrating \( 2\pi\,([[m]]y) \).
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( 2\pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region bounded above by the line \( y = [[k]] \) and below by the parabola \( y = x^{2} \), for \( 0 \le x \le [[s]] \), is revolved about the \( y \)-axis. Using cylindrical shells, find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: A shell at radius \( x \) has height equal to the vertical gap between the curves, \( [[k]] - x^{2} \), so the volume is \( \int_{0}^{[[s]]} 2\pi x\,([[k]] - x^{2})\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates the shell height only at \( x=[[s]] \) instead of integrating.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This treats the region as a disk of constant radius, with no calculus basis.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( 2\pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region bounded by the curve \( y = \dfrac{[[k]]}{x} \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \) is revolved about the \( y \)-axis. Using cylindrical shells, find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: A shell at radius \( x \) has height \( \dfrac{[[k]]}{x} \), so the shell's surface area \( 2\pi x \cdot \dfrac{[[k]]}{x} = 2\pi[[k]] \) is CONSTANT. The volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} 2\pi[[k]]\,dx = 2\pi[[k]]([[b]]-[[a]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This mistakenly keeps a factor of \( x \) after it should have canceled with the height.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This uses the disk method instead of shells.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( 2\pi \) from the correct volume.
